text.title: rag-eval-production-monitoring Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of rag-eval-production-monitoring enforces.

rule.statement: Log latency, source count, top-1 chunk id, and user feedback on every query. No LLM-judge in the per-query path.
rule.rationale: LLM judging at full traffic costs too much; lightweight signals are enough for anomaly detection.

rule.statement: Aggregate over a rolling window (default 24h, configurable); never alert from a single-query reading.
rule.rationale: Single-query spikes are noise; window aggregation surfaces real shifts.

rule.statement: Calibrate anomaly thresholds against 7-30 days of production data; never ship hardcoded defaults.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded thresholds either flood with false positives or miss real drift.

rule.statement: Schedule LLM-judge on a daily 5-10% sample; record judge_model + sampling seed.
rule.rationale: Sampling keeps cost bounded while still catching faithfulness drift.

rule.statement: When anomalies fire, link to the latest batch-eval report so on-call has a reproducible diagnosis.
rule.rationale: Without this link, on-call has to manually find the most recent batch report — slows down triage.

rule.statement: Skip when traffic is too low for windowed aggregation to be statistically meaningful (< 50 queries/day).
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
